creator: sheldonh@uunet.co.za
text:
On Wed, 26 May 1999 22:15:22 +0200, Arjan de Vet wrote:

> The string $PATH:$ac_aux_path_sendmail$ac_dummy doesn't get split
> correctly on the colons for some reason.  This is with a FreeBSD
> 3.2-RELEASE /bin/sh.

I can't for the life of me reproduce the problem, but I'd appreciate it
if you'd try patching the port's patches/patch04 with the diff below and
let me know how a ``make clean install'' fairs.

Ciao,
Sheldon.

Index: patch-04
===================================================================
RCS file: /home/ncvs/ports/mail/mutt/patches/patch-04,v
retrieving revision 1.4
diff -u -d -r1.4 patch-04
--- patch-04	1999/02/13 20:15:12	1.4
+++ patch-04	1999/05/27 09:23:03
@@ -1,5 +1,14 @@
 --- configure.orig.bak	Fri Feb 12 13:59:51 1999
 +++ configure	Sat Feb 13 18:00:01 1999
+@@ -1156,7 +1156,7 @@
+   ;;
+   *)
+   IFS="${IFS= 	}"; ac_save_ifs="$IFS"; IFS="${IFS}:"
+-  for ac_dir in $PATH:/usr/sbin:/usr/lib$ac_dummy; do
++  for ac_dir in `echo $PATH:/usr/sbin:/usr/lib$ac_dummy|tr ':' ' '`; do
+     test -z "$ac_dir" && ac_dir=.
+     if test -f $ac_dir/$ac_word; then
+       ac_cv_path_SENDMAIL="$ac_dir/$ac_word"
 @@ -1725,6 +1725,9 @@
  /* We use char because int might match the return type of a gcc2
      builtin and then its argument prototype would still apply.  */
